questions.question:
The paper as shown in the figure is folded to make a cube where each square corresponds to a particular face of the cube. Which one of the following options correctly represents the cube?

Note: The figures shown are representative.

The net is a cross/plus shape with the following face symbols:
- Top square: filled circle (dot)
- Center square: hollow triangle (open delta)
- Right of center: filled triangle
- Bottom square: open circle (hollow)
- Left of center: blank
- Far right (second column): blank

A. Cube showing top face with filled dot; front face with hollow triangle (open delta)
B. Cube showing top face with filled dot; front face with hollow triangle; right face with filled triangle (solid)
C. Cube showing front face with hollow triangle; bottom face with filled triangle
D. Cube showing top face with filled triangle; front face with hollow triangle; right face with filled dot
questions.answer: A. Cube showing top face with filled dot; front face with hollow triangle (open delta)
questions.to_find: which cube view correctly represents the folded paper net

understand.plain: A flat paper net in the shape of a cross is printed with different symbols on each of the six faces. When folded, each square becomes one face of a cube, and the spatial relationships between faces must be preserved. The task is to identify which 3D cube view is consistent with the adjacency and opposition constraints of the given net. Opposite faces in a cross net (top-center-bottom column) are: top is opposite to bottom; left is opposite to right.

keywords.explain: A flat 2D arrangement of six connected squares that folds into a cube without overlap. The spatial position of each square in the net determines which faces are adjacent or opposite.
keywords.example: A plus/cross-shaped net has one center square, with four squares attached to each of its four sides, and one square sticking out from one of those.

keywords.explain: In a cube, opposite faces never share an edge. In a cross net, the top square and bottom square of the main column are opposite to each other; the left and right squares in a row are also paired.
keywords.example: In this net, the filled dot (top) is opposite to the open circle (bottom); the blank left face is opposite to the filled triangle (right of center).

keywords.explain: Faces that share an edge on the net remain adjacent (sharing an edge) on the cube. The center face is adjacent to all four of its direct neighbors.
keywords.example: The hollow triangle (center) is adjacent to: filled dot (top), open circle (bottom), blank (left), filled triangle (right) -- all four sides of the center square.

understand.visual_alt: Cross-shaped paper net with filled dot on top, hollow triangle in center, filled triangle to the right, hollow circle at bottom, and blank faces on left and far right.

given.aim: Identify which cube option correctly shows the folded net, preserving all face adjacencies and oppositions.

terms.meaning: Symbol on the top square of the cross net; this face will be opposite to the hollow circle (bottom square) when folded.
terms.example: Dot face is never adjacent to the circle face; they end up on opposite sides of the cube.
terms.connects: opposite to hollow circle; adjacent to hollow triangle, blank-left, solid-triangle, and blank-right

terms.meaning: Symbol on the center square; it is adjacent to all four faces directly connected to it in the net (dot, circle, blank-left, solid-triangle).
terms.example: This face will appear as the front face in the correct cube view.
terms.connects: adjacent to dot(top), circle(bottom), blank(left), solid-triangle(right)

terms.meaning: Symbol on the bottom square of the column; opposite to the dot (top square) once folded.
terms.example: If you can see the circle face in a view, the dot face must be on the hidden back side.
terms.connects: opposite to dot; adjacent to hollow triangle, blank-left, solid-triangle, blank-right

terms.meaning: Symbol on the square to the right of the center; opposite to the blank-left face.
terms.example: Solid triangle and blank face never appear on opposite ends of a single axis together.
terms.connects: opposite to blank-left; adjacent to hollow triangle, dot, circle

given.plan: Map each face's position in the net to its cube face. Determine opposite pairs: (dot, circle), (blank-left, solid-triangle), (blank-far-right, hollow-triangle). Then verify which cube view shows only mutually adjacent faces visible and in correct relative orientation.

steps.title: Identify opposite face pairs from the net
steps.formula_raw: In a cross net (column of 3 + 2 wings), opposite pairs = (top, bottom), (left-wing, right-wing), (far-right extension, center)
steps.apply: Column top-to-bottom: dot -> hollow-triangle -> circle. So dot is opposite to circle. Left wing (blank) is opposite to right wing (solid-triangle). The far-right extension (blank-2) is opposite to the center (hollow-triangle).
steps.note: Two squares separated by exactly one square in the same column are opposite faces.

steps.title: List all adjacencies of the hollow triangle (center) face
steps.formula_raw: A square in the net is adjacent to all squares sharing a direct edge with it.
steps.apply: Hollow-triangle (center) shares edges with: dot (above), circle (below), blank-left (left), solid-triangle (right). So all four neighbors are adjacent to the hollow-triangle face. The only face NOT adjacent to hollow-triangle is blank-far-right (which is its opposite).
steps.note: This means if the front face is hollow-triangle, valid visible faces include dot, circle, blank-left, and solid-triangle -- but not blank-far-right.

steps.title: Check Option A
steps.formula_raw: Cube view validity: top, front, and right faces must all be mutually adjacent (no two can be opposite).
steps.apply: Option A shows: top = dot, front = hollow-triangle. Dot and hollow-triangle are adjacent (they share an edge in the net). This is a valid adjacency. The visible right face in Option A is blank (no symbol), which is also adjacent to hollow-triangle. No opposite pair is visible simultaneously. Option A is consistent.
steps.note: Option A correctly shows dot on top and hollow triangle on front, matching the net adjacency.

steps.title: Eliminate incorrect options
steps.formula_raw: An option is invalid if it shows two opposite faces simultaneously or places adjacent faces in positions inconsistent with the net.
steps.apply: Option B places solid-triangle as the right face when front is hollow-triangle and top is dot -- but solid-triangle is adjacent to hollow-triangle, so this adjacency is valid in isolation. However the orientation of the solid-triangle relative to hollow-triangle must match the net (right side), and the combined view must be consistent. Option C places solid-triangle at bottom when hollow-triangle is front -- circle should be at bottom (opposite dot on top), not solid-triangle. Option D places solid-triangle on top -- but dot is on top in the net, not solid-triangle. Only Option A is fully consistent.
steps.note: Always verify all three visible faces are pairwise adjacent and their relative positions match the net.

solution.result: Option A correctly represents the cube. The top face shows the filled dot, the front face shows the hollow triangle, and the visible side faces are consistent with the net's adjacency structure.

questions.formula_note: Paper folding questions use spatial reasoning rather than algebraic formulas; the key rules are opposite-face identification and pairwise adjacency checking.
